title: The One with the Boob Job

jokes.text: What? What? -Shaving? -No, rabies.
jokes.setup: Monica asks what's wrong after seeing Joey bleeding
jokes.punchline: Joey deadpans 'rabies' as if it's obvious
jokes.explanation: Joey's matter-of-fact delivery of an absurd diagnosis creates a perfect deadpan moment. The economy is excellent - maximum funny per word.

jokes.text: ...farting on my lap. Not so funny when someone does that to you, is it?
jokes.setup: Ross gets sentimental about Emma growing up
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'precious memory' was her farting on him
jokes.explanation: Standard baby humor - subverts the sentimental moment but relies on bodily function comedy

jokes.text: Who's painting us?
jokes.setup: Everyone stands perfectly still watching Emma
jokes.punchline: Joey's confusion about their frozen poses
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - four words that perfectly capture Joey's confusion and the visual absurdity of their tableau

jokes.text: ...since I was a kid and they came up with Double Stuf Oreos.
jokes.setup: Monica comparing their financial hit to her savings history
jokes.punchline: Her childhood savings were decimated by cookie purchases
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica character moment - her obsessive relationship with food extends to childhood financial decisions

jokes.text: ...but I'm inclined to blame Enron.
jokes.setup: Monica doesn't know exactly what happened to their money
jokes.punchline: Blames the corporate scandal du jour
jokes.explanation: Topical reference that was timely but doesn't hold up well. Generic 'blame the corporate villain' joke

jokes.text: Although it would be nice if the thing that you loved was finding gold.
jokes.setup: Monica supports Chandler doing what he loves
jokes.punchline: Wishes his passion was more financially practical
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Monica's practical nature showing through her supportiveness

jokes.text: And maybe we start eating out of Joey's refrigerator for a change.
jokes.setup: Monica suggesting cost-cutting measures
jokes.punchline: Reversing the usual dynamic of Joey eating their food
jokes.explanation: Nice reversal of established group dynamic. 'For a change' sells the irony well

jokes.text: You're a chef. What can you make out of baking soda and beer?
jokes.setup: Monica's suggestion to eat from Joey's fridge
jokes.punchline: Chandler knows Joey's fridge contains only these two items
jokes.explanation: Great specificity - baking soda and beer are hilariously minimal ingredients for a chef to work with

jokes.text: Or, you slipped on ice at their house last month. Can we sue them?
jokes.setup: Chandler suggests borrowing from his parents
jokes.punchline: Monica immediately pivots to lawsuit possibility
jokes.explanation: Monica's competitive/scheming nature showing - she'd rather sue than ask for help

jokes.text: If there's one thing my father taught me, it was... well, to always knock before going into the pool house.
jokes.setup: Chandler starts to share wisdom from his father
jokes.punchline: The 'wisdom' is about avoiding awkward encounters
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection structure and great character integration - reveals Chandler's dysfunctional family in a funny way

jokes.text: ...even if it means you working twice as hard.
jokes.setup: Chandler declares he'll be the man and get them through this
jokes.punchline: His solution is Monica working harder, not him
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Chandler's self-serving logic disguised as heroism

jokes.text: No, I don't-- Get him. Get him! Separate the sick one from the herd!
jokes.setup: Mike notes Phoebe loves watching animals fight despite not eating them
jokes.punchline: Phoebe denies it while enthusiastically cheering for violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction between Phoebe's stated beliefs and actions. The escalation from denial to bloodthirst is well-crafted

jokes.text: I put that tube top on as a joke.
jokes.setup: Mike offers Phoebe clothes to wear
jokes.punchline: Reveals he tried on her tube top
jokes.explanation: Standard cross-dressing humor. The defensive 'as a joke' makes it slightly better

jokes.text: Why don't you turn them inside-- Done it.
jokes.setup: Mike complains about overused boxers
jokes.punchline: He's already turned them inside out
jokes.explanation: Good economy with the quick cutoff. Gross-out humor but well-executed timing

jokes.text: Oh, fine. Yeah, you got away. But don't look so smug. You're gonna bleed to death.
jokes.setup: Phoebe was watching nature documentary
jokes.punchline: She's invested enough to trash-talk the escaping prey
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Phoebe's competitive nature extends to TV animals. 'Don't look so smug' is perfect

jokes.text: Partly because I live above a known crack den...
jokes.setup: Mike explains why he hates going home
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions living above drug dealers
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of serious information as throwaway detail

jokes.text: Wow, Mike Hanigan... you sure know how to make a girl say 'Hell, yeah.'
jokes.setup: Mike asks if she wants to move in together
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's enthusiastic but oddly phrased acceptance
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's unique way of expressing excitement. The full name usage adds formality to the crude response

jokes.text: Pick up your socks. Put down the toilet seat. No, we are not having sex anymore.
jokes.setup: Phoebe says she's always wanted to live with a guy
jokes.punchline: Immediately lists stereotypical relationship complaints
jokes.explanation: Standard relationship humor but good escalation structure. The enthusiasm-to-complaints switch is well-timed

jokes.text: A man's ass is not nudity!
jokes.setup: Joey reading TV Guide
jokes.punchline: His passionate defense of male rear nudity standards
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his actor's perspective on nudity standards. The passion in his voice makes it funnier

jokes.text: I'd be lying if I said I hadn't thought about it myself. But Chandler is my best friend. It would be wrong. Good, but wrong.
jokes.setup: Monica asks Joey about something secret from Chandler
jokes.punchline: Joey thinks she's propositioning him
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect Joey logic. 'Good, but wrong' is a fantastic line that captures his moral confusion

jokes.text: Okay, first of all, it would be great.
jokes.setup: Joey realizes Monica wants money, not sex
jokes.punchline: Still maintains it would be great
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the misdirection. Joey's ego won't let him back down completely

jokes.text: What do you think I am, a soap opera star? Yeah. That's right, I am.
jokes.setup: Monica asks for $2000
jokes.punchline: Joey's rhetorical question accidentally reminds him he IS wealthy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-correction structure. Perfect Joey character moment where his own rhetorical question changes his mind. Meta humor about his actor status.

jokes.text: Chandler? Pride? I know, he thinks he has it. It's really cute.
jokes.setup: Monica says it would hurt Chandler's pride
jokes.punchline: Joey finds Chandler's pride endearing rather than real
jokes.explanation: Great character insight - Joey sees through Chandler's persona with affectionate condescension

jokes.text: Whoops, Joey fell down the stairs. Or, 'Whoops, Joey electrocuted himself again.'
jokes.setup: Joey complains about modern baby-proofing obsession
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions his repeated childhood accidents
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character backstory that explains Joey's current personality. 'Again' suggests this was routine. Dark but delivered lightly.

jokes.text: Women can. You can't.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks if he thinks women can't do handiwork
jokes.punchline: Joey makes it specific to Rachel personally
jokes.explanation: Great economy - six words that perfectly thread the needle between political correctness and honesty

jokes.text: There is a hardware store right down the street?
jokes.setup: Rachel brags about finding the hardware store alone
jokes.punchline: Discovers there was an easier option right nearby
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's obliviousness to practical matters

jokes.text: Unless they're playing 'This Is What My Sister Would Look Like.' Yeah, she's not so cute.
jokes.setup: Mike says guys don't steal makeup like girls do
jokes.punchline: Admits to using makeup to imagine his sister
jokes.explanation: Weird and specific enough to be funny. The follow-up insult about his sister's looks adds another layer

jokes.text: Is that a bit you guys do? Uh-huh. We're playing you two.
jokes.setup: Ross asks about Mike and Phoebe's domestic routine
jokes.punchline: They're consciously parodying Monica and Chandler
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about relationship stereotypes within the show's own dynamics

jokes.text: I'm a scientist. I observe.
jokes.setup: Ross compliments Mike's eyes
jokes.punchline: Defensive explanation for noticing another man's attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross moment - using his profession to justify awkward social situation. Great economy and character integration

jokes.text: ...that highlights my breasts in an obvious yet classy way.
jokes.setup: Phoebe fantasizes about her wedding dress
jokes.punchline: Very specific requirements about breast presentation
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe moment - her blunt approach to traditionally romantic topics. 'Obvious yet classy' is a nice contradiction

jokes.text: Oh, my God, load up the Volvo, I want to be a soccer mom.
jokes.setup: Phoebe realizes she wants conventional family life
jokes.punchline: Embraces the most stereotypical suburban mom image
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - free-spirited Phoebe embracing ultimate conventionality. 'Load up the Volvo' is perfectly specific

jokes.text: Don't you mean 'groomie'?
jokes.setup: Mike calls Phoebe 'roomie'
jokes.punchline: Monica's portmanteau of groom and roomie
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that the show itself acknowledges as terrible. Forced and predictable

jokes.text: Honey, leave the terrible jokes to me.
jokes.setup: Monica makes the 'groomie' pun
jokes.punchline: Mike acknowledges his own bad joke reputation
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment that salvages the previous weak joke by acknowledging it

jokes.text: Wow, seriously. Can't do this.
jokes.setup: Rachel tries to install baby-proofing equipment
jokes.punchline: Her defeated admission after failing
jokes.explanation: Pays off Joey's earlier skepticism. The defeated tone sells Rachel's realization

jokes.text: That is the fastest I have ever thought.
jokes.setup: Joey correctly deduces Chandler's financial situation
jokes.punchline: Joey's amazement at his own reasoning abilities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey self-awareness moment. The pride in basic deduction is endearing and funny

jokes.text: I am on fire.
jokes.setup: Joey correctly guesses the $2000 amount
jokes.punchline: His continued amazement at his mental prowess
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Joey's intellectual pride. Works as callback to establish his temporary genius streak

jokes.text: Boob job.
jokes.setup: Chandler wonders what Monica would spend $2000 on secretly
jokes.punchline: Joey's matter-of-fact revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words that completely shift the scene's direction. Joey's blunt delivery is character-appropriate

jokes.text: Making them smaller, that would be crazy.
jokes.setup: Chandler says Monica doesn't need a boob job
jokes.punchline: Joey clarifies what would be truly crazy
jokes.explanation: Good Joey logic - he has very specific opinions about breast enhancement priorities

jokes.text: I don't have any porn. Back of the closet in my bedroom.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks about his porn stash
jokes.punchline: Mike denies having any then immediately reveals location
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - the contradiction happens so quickly it's almost subconscious honesty overriding denial

jokes.text: Well, I think the reason people laughed is that it's a play on the word 'roomie.'
jokes.setup: Mike mentions Monica's 'groomie' joke
jokes.punchline: Over-explains the obvious wordplay
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about explaining obvious jokes. Mike's earnestness makes it funnier

jokes.text: I get the joke. Sophisticated as it was.
jokes.setup: Mike over-explains the 'groomie' pun
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's sarcastic praise of the joke's complexity
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic follow-up. Phoebe's dry delivery of 'sophisticated' works well

jokes.text: Okay, well, that's bad. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Mike starts to describe his wife's extreme behavior
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's understated reaction to something clearly horrific
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated reaction to implied extreme behavior. The pause and matter-of-fact delivery work well

jokes.text: Perhaps... a blond who always uses the toilet? Except for once in the ocean.
jokes.setup: Phoebe suggests Mike might feel differently with someone else
jokes.punchline: Her one exception to perfect toilet behavior
jokes.explanation: Great Phoebe moment - starts with self-promotion then admits to ocean incident. The specificity makes it funnier

jokes.text: Yeah. I don't know who I was kidding. I can barely use chopsticks.
jokes.setup: Ross asks if Rachel called for help with baby-proofing
jokes.punchline: Rachel's admission with comparison to chopsticks
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment. Chopsticks comparison adds relatable scale to her incompetence

jokes.text: Moths?
jokes.setup: Ross asks what happened to his sweater sleeve
jokes.punchline: Rachel's implausible excuse delivered innocently
jokes.explanation: Good economy - one word that's clearly a lie but delivered with innocent confusion

jokes.text: And definitely not... two single things.
jokes.setup: Chandler says he wouldn't change anything about Rachel
jokes.punchline: His awkward emphasis on 'two things' (breasts)
jokes.explanation: Chandler's attempt to be subtle about breasts. The awkward delivery makes his discomfort funny

jokes.text: I am warm... for your form.
jokes.setup: Chandler tries to compliment Rachel's body
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at poetic body compliment
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler awkwardness. The pause before 'for your form' shows his discomfort with directness

jokes.text: Don't answer that.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks if Rachel wants any part of him to get bigger
jokes.punchline: Immediate panic about potential honest answer
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment where Chandler realizes he's opened himself up to criticism

jokes.text: Just when you thought that dude couldn't get any weirder.
jokes.setup: Joey observes Chandler's strange behavior with Rachel
jokes.punchline: His assessment of escalating weirdness
jokes.explanation: Good outside perspective on Chandler's behavior. Joey as the voice of reason is inherently funny

jokes.text: My God, he sounded just like my high-school wrestling coach.
jokes.setup: Rachel analyzes Chandler's 'don't get bigger' comments
jokes.punchline: Disturbing comparison to presumably inappropriate coach
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that implies inappropriate coaching behavior. The specificity makes it more disturbing and funny

jokes.text: God, it's just such a magical time.
jokes.setup: Phoebe lists all the awful physical changes of pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic summary of pregnancy as 'magical'
jokes.explanation: Perfect sarcastic cap to the list of pregnancy horrors. Phoebe's deadpan delivery sells the irony

jokes.text: Seriously, you? 'Divorce-o'?
jokes.setup: Ross says he loves marriage despite his failures
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's unflattering nickname for Ross
jokes.explanation: Good nickname that captures Ross's marriage history. Phoebe's blunt naming style

jokes.text: If you have to call me a name, I'd prefer 'Ross, the Divorcer.' It's just cooler.
jokes.setup: Phoebe calls him 'Divorce-o'
jokes.punchline: Ross's attempt to rebrand his failures more heroically
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Ross trying to make his failures sound badass. The earnestness makes it funnier

jokes.text: And once afterward.
jokes.setup: Ross mentions good times with Carol before she became lesbian
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of post-lesbian encounter
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - three words that reveal so much about their relationship. Ross's casualness makes it funnier

jokes.text: It's not cooler.
jokes.setup: Ross settles on 'The Divorcer' as his nickname
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's flat assessment after he tests it out
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Ross's attempt at coolness. Phoebe's bluntness punctures his fantasy

jokes.text: Fascist.
jokes.setup: Chandler says he loves her breasts the way they are
jokes.punchline: Rachel's extreme political comparison
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - one word escalation to political extremism. Rachel's over-the-top reaction is perfect

jokes.text: Your ass?!
jokes.setup: Rachel mentions her ass will get bigger during pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Chandler's horrified realization
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Chandler's horror. The exclamation marks sell his panic

jokes.text: ...but the giant ass and the big clown feet?
jokes.setup: Chandler considers accepting breast changes
jokes.punchline: His horror at the other physical changes
jokes.explanation: 'Big clown feet' is a hilariously specific and visual description. Good escalation of his shallow priorities

jokes.text: What?
jokes.setup: Monica describes childbirth reality
jokes.punchline: Chandler's delayed processing of pregnancy implications
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat. Chandler's slow realization is relatable

jokes.text: Okay, so I'm out $4000 and nobody's boobs are getting any bigger?
jokes.setup: The money lending confusion is resolved
jokes.punchline: Joey's focus on the lack of breast enhancement outcome
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey priorities - he's most disappointed by the lack of breast enhancement. Great callback resolution

jokes.text: Hey. Hey, what do you guys think about this: Ross, the Divorce Force.
jokes.setup: Ross continues working on his nickname
jokes.punchline: His latest attempt at making divorce sound cool
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Ross's nickname obsession. 'Divorce Force' sounds like a superhero team

jokes.text: They are awfully boxy.
jokes.setup: Ross questions if Phoebe can picture herself in a Volvo
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's practical assessment of Volvo design
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe moment - she gets distracted by practical car critique instead of relationship implications

jokes.text: You know, sometimes your words, they hurt.
jokes.setup: Phoebe makes sarcastic comment about moving difficulty
jokes.punchline: Mike's mock-wounded response
jokes.explanation: Mild relationship humor. The mock sensitivity is appropriately understated

jokes.text: I mean, not for me, because I'm only pretending to hold it, but for these guys.
jokes.setup: Joey asks where to put heavy furniture while helping move
jokes.punchline: Admits he's not actually helping carry it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his casual admission of laziness while maintaining the appearance of helping

jokes.text: And...hernia.
jokes.setup: Mike realizes Phoebe wants marriage while he's holding heavy furniture
jokes.punchline: His physical reaction to emotional revelation
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy - the emotional shock causing literal physical damage. Perfect timing

jokes.text: I said to bring the cushions up first, but no.
jokes.setup: Serious relationship conversation happening during move
jokes.punchline: Joey's focus on moving logistics during emotional moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey priorities - more concerned with moving strategy than relationship drama around him

jokes.text: Look on the bright side. At least you won't have to live with this ugly chair.
jokes.setup: Everyone comforting Phoebe after breakup
jokes.punchline: Monica's typically inappropriate focus on decor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - trying to find practical silver lining in emotional devastation

jokes.text: That was here already, huh?
jokes.setup: Monica insults Mike's furniture after breakup
jokes.punchline: Ross's awkward realization of timing
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment recognition. Ross's social awareness coming too late

jokes.text: Who knows when I'll be able to get in here again, huh?
jokes.setup: Joey taking advantage of Phoebe's emotional state to raid Mike's fridge
jokes.punchline: His casual opportunism during others' pain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey selfishness - taking practical advantage of emotional situation without malice

tmdb_overview: While reviewing their monthly expenses, Monica and Chandler discover that their situation is less than stellar. This results in Monica asking Joey to lend her some money without telling Chandler.
